title: Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Madhya Pradesh

heroImage.alt: Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary, Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Veerangana Durgawati Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Damoh district and Sagar district of Madhya Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Veerangana Durgawati Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Damoh district and Sagar district of Madhya Pradesh, India. It was declared as a wildlife sanctuary in 1996. In 2023, it was declared as a tiger reserve with a total area of 2,339.12 square kilometres (903.14 sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Jabera
topPlaces.description: Jabera is a census town city in Damoh district, Madhya Pradesh, India. Jabera is a tehsil headquarter situated on the Damoh-Jabalpur Road.

topPlaces.name: Singrampur
topPlaces.description: Singrampur is a historical place where final battle fought between Rani Durgawati and Mughal king Akbar's Senapati Asaf Khan. The town in named after Sangram which in Hindi means war.

topPlaces.name: Vindhya Range
topPlaces.description: The Vindhya Range (IPA: [ʋɪnd̪ʱjə], also known as Vindhyachal) is a complex, discontinuous chain of mountain ridges, hill ranges, highlands and plateau escarpments in west-central India. Technically, the Vindhyas do not form a single mountain range in the geological sense.

items.note: Jabera is a census town city in Damoh district, Madhya Pradesh, India. Jabera is a tehsil headquarter situated on the Damoh-Jabalpur Road.
Singrampur is a historical place where final battle fought between Rani Durgawati and Mughal king Akbar's Senapati Asaf Khan. The town in named after Sangram which in Hindi means war.

The Vindhya Range (IPA: [ʋɪnd̪ʱjə], also known as Vindhyachal) is a complex, discontinuous chain of mountain ridges, hill ranges, highlands and plateau escarpments in west-central India. Technically, the Vindhyas do not form a single mountain range in the geological sense.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jabalpur Airport (53 km) and the nearest railway station is Jabalpur Junction (47 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Veerangana Durgavati Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jabera, Singrampur, Vindhya Range.
